description: Phase 5 - Architecture Documentation and Migration Guide: Create comprehensive documentation covering the full architecture, migration steps from Cloudflare to AWS, cost analysis (Free Tier breakdown), and operational runbook. Also create an environment configuration system for the frontend to switch between local dev and production AWS endpoints.
steps: Create docs/architecture.md with: Architecture diagram (text-based), component descriptions, data flow for authenticated vs anonymous users, DynamoDB access patterns, security model (Cognito + API Gateway authorizer)

Create docs/migration-guide.md with: Step-by-step migration from Cloudflare Workers to AWS (parallel running period, DNS cutover plan, rollback procedure), prerequisites (AWS account, domain, Google OAuth app registration)

Create docs/aws-free-tier.md with: Detailed cost analysis showing all services used and their Free Tier limits (S3: 5GB, CloudFront: 1TB/month, Lambda: 1M requests, DynamoDB: 25GB/25WCU/25RCU, Cognito: 50K MAUs, API Gateway: 1M calls), monthly cost estimate for typical quiz app traffic, alerts configuration for billing
Create docs/runbook.md with: Monitoring setup (CloudWatch basics), troubleshooting common issues, how to update content (questions), how to deploy manually, how to manage Cognito users
Create src/config/environment.ts with typed environment configuration: API_URL, COGNITO_USER_POOL_ID, COGNITO_CLIENT_ID, COGNITO_DOMAIN - sourced from Vite env variables (import.meta.env)
Create .env.development and .env.production templates for Vite environment variables (VITE_API_URL, VITE_COGNITO_USER_POOL_ID, etc.)
Update root README.md to reference all new documentation and describe the evolved architecture
acceptance_criteria: All documentation files are well-structured markdown with clear sections
Architecture document describes the full system (frontend, auth, API, database)
Migration guide provides actionable steps a developer can follow
Cost analysis accurately reflects AWS Free Tier limits for all services used
Environment configuration compiles and exports typed config object
Vite env files document all required variables
npm run typecheck passes with new environment config module
findings: Pre-existing test failure in src/data/__tests__/questionStore.test.ts (getQuestionById test) - unrelated to this feature. All .env files required .gitignore exceptions since the project ignores .env.* by default. Added !.env.development and !.env.production to .gitignore.
